news.headline: NeurAxis Declares Q2 2026 Stock Dividend on Series B Preferred Stock
news.summary: NeurAxis's board declared a Q2 2026 dividend on its Series B Preferred Stock payable entirely in roughly 80,463 shares of common stock (rather than cash) on July 29, 2026 to holders of record as of July 21, 2026. The equity-settled dividend conserves cash but modestly dilutes common shareholders.

news.headline: NeurAxis Stockholders Approve Directors and Expanded Equity Compensation Plans
news.summary: At its June 10, 2026 annual meeting, NeurAxis shareholders re-elected all six director nominees and approved expanding the 2022 Omnibus Incentive Plan's share pool from about 1.48 million to 3.4 million shares, plus a new 2025 Employee Stock Purchase Plan.

news.headline: NeurAxis Reports Record First-Quarter 2026 Financial Results Driven by 80% Revenue Growth
news.summary: Q1 2026 revenue rose 80% year-over-year to $1.6 million on 35% higher unit deliveries, with gross margin expanding to 86.4% and operating loss improving 24%, largely reflecting the new AMA Category I CPT code for PENFS effective January 1, 2026. Cash stood at $7.1 million as of March 31, 2026, though the going-concern qualification from the FY2025 audit persisted.

news.headline: Bigger Capital Fund Discloses 6.56% Passive Stake in NeurAxis
news.summary: Bigger Capital Fund, LP filed an amended Schedule 13G reporting beneficial ownership of 755,577 NRXS shares (about 6.56% of shares outstanding) as of May 1, 2026, reaffirming it as one of the company's larger institutional holders.

news.headline: NeurAxis Expands Payer Coverage with Four New Medical Policies
news.summary: NeurAxis announced four additional insurer medical policies covering PENFS/IB-Stim, adding roughly 1.25 million covered lives across West Virginia, New Hampshire, South Carolina, Virginia and Florida. The move continues the company's push to broaden reimbursement access ahead of the Category I CPT code taking effect.

news.headline: NeurAxis Announces Preferred Stock Dividend
news.summary: The board declared the Q1 2026 stock dividend on Series B Preferred Stock, continuing the company's practice of settling preferred dividend obligations in common shares rather than cash to preserve liquidity ahead of the repeat declaration in July 2026.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings call
catalysts.detail: NeurAxis is scheduled to report second-quarter 2026 financial results and host a business update call.

catalysts.label: Series B preferred stock dividend payment
catalysts.detail: The already-declared Q2 2026 stock dividend (~80,463 common shares) pays out to Series B preferred holders of record as of July 21, 2026.

catalysts.label: Continued Medicaid/commercial payer coverage expansion
catalysts.detail: NeurAxis has been announcing new insurer medical policies roughly every 4-8 weeks since the AMA Category I CPT code took effect (Jan 1, 2026), pushing total covered lives past ~100 million; further state Medicaid fee-schedule additions and commercial payer policies are expected to keep expanding IB-Stim's addressable, reimbursable market.

catalysts.label: VA Federal Supply Schedule commercial ramp
catalysts.detail: Following its VA FSS contract (effective December 1, 2025), NeurAxis is scaling IB-Stim access within the Veterans Affairs system (~7 million patients); management has said even ~0.25% penetration in 2026 could add $2M+ in incremental revenue.

catalysts.label: Path to cash-flow breakeven
catalysts.detail: Management has guided to roughly $1M or less in monthly cash burn for the remainder of 2026 and cited approximately $15 million in annualized revenue as the level needed for cash-flow breakeven; this remains a key milestone investors are tracking given the company's going-concern disclosure and reliance on ATM/warrant financing.

pulse: NeurAxis (NYSE American: NRXS) is a small-cap medtech commercializing IB-Stim, an FDA-cleared PENFS neuromodulation device for pediatric functional abdominal pain/IBS and functional dyspepsia, riding a new AMA Category I CPT code (effective January 1, 2026) that has rapidly expanded insurance coverage and driven strong revenue growth (Q1 2026 revenue +80% YoY to $1.6M, gross margin 86.4%). The company remains pre-profitability with a going-concern qualification and depends on ATM equity sales, warrant exercises and Series B preferred financing (settled in stock, most recently July 29) to fund operations, though cash burn has been narrowing and analysts (Craig-Hallum, Buy, $13 PT) are increasingly bullish on VA Federal Supply Schedule and Medicaid channel expansion. Near-term, the stock's trajectory into the Q2 2026 earnings call (scheduled September 17) will be the next test of whether reimbursement-driven growth can carry the company toward its self-described ~$15M revenue cash-flow-breakeven target.
